response: solution The primary error in the erroneous code is likely a failure to handle duplicate elements by separating them into odd and even indices after sorting, causing adjacent equal values when the median appears too frequently. solution A robust approach involves sorting the array, then placing the smaller half into even indices and the larger half into odd indices in reverse order to minimize adjacent duplicates. solution For the follow-up requirement of O(n) time without full sorting, we can use the Quickselect algorithm to find the median, then partition the array into elements less than, equal to, and greater than the median before interleaving them. solution This median-based partitioning strategy ensures that even with negative integers, the relative ordering is preserved and adjacent equality is avoided whenever the input guarantees a valid wiggle sort exists. solution The final implementation correctly transforms [1,1,1,4,5,6] into [1,4,1,5,1,6] by strategically distributing the three 1s across non-adjacent positions.
